title: THIS Is Why Civilization May Have Started Much EARLIER Than We Think

transcript.text: the very idea that we take is complete
Dogma That civilization started in the
Fertile Crescent six thousand years ago
that concept was only established 200
years ago with the discovery of the
great capital cities of Assyria and
Babylonia as we explore and discover
more ancient archaeological sites our
dates for civilization get progressively
older and older
the Beckley Tepe an ancient burial site
in modern turkey dating as far back as
9000 BC and involving the complex
assembly of 50 ton blocks the Tepe was
excavated in the 90s so I think it would
actually be the easy or safe bet to say
that in the next hundred years we'll
find architecture that predates even
this it's like a sort of Moore's law for
archeology the deeper we go the older
human history gets the lowest Parts in
any sediment layer are the hardest parts
to explore that's just geology 101 and
our digging an underground mapping
techniques are getting better and better
